title: Earnings call transcript: TheRealReal posts Q1 2026 EPS beat, stock edges up
text: TheRealReal exceeded analyst expectations in Q1 2026, delivering an EPS of -$0.01 versus the forecasted -$0.05—an 80% earnings beat. The company reported revenue of $190 million, surpassing the anticipated $188.03 million. Gross Merchandise Value (GMV): $606 million, up 24% year-over-year. Revenue growth: 19% YoY. Adjusted EBITDA: $13.1 million (6.9% margin), expanding 430 basis points. Average Order Value: $646, up 15% YoY. Active buyers (trailing 12-month): Up 10% YoY. The stock gained 1.85% in after-hours trading to $13.24, reflecting investor confidence in operational execution and growth trajectory. Management raised full-year 2026 expectations: GMV $2.42-$2.47 billion (14%-16% growth), revenue $770-$784 million (11%-13% growth), adjusted EBITDA $59-$67 million (8.1% margin target). CEO Rati Levesque stated the company is compounding advantages through three strategic pillars: growth playbook, service obsession, and operational excellence. The flywheel dynamic strengthens as 43% of new consigners convert from existing buyers. Management emphasized the luxury resale market's mainstream trajectory, with nearly half of luxury consumers now considering resale value in purchasing decisions.
